items.criterion: The PR implements KUSABI-CORE-MVP-MEMORY-HISTORICAL-SQLITE-ARCHIVE-REVIEW-001 and does not substitute runtime remediation, archive execution, migration, deletion, release, or publish work.
All changed files remain within the Cell allowed paths, with no src, package, workflow, deploy, local config, DB content, docs/v2, schema, fixture, runner, branch protection, or ruleset edits.

The Cell anchors to issue #228 corrected policy: retained connected/development agents use shared PostgreSQL, stale aliases are inactive/archive, historical SQLite residue is reviewed separately, and SQLite exceptions remain FX-only.
The review contract lists the seven remaining non-binding SQLite files and separates zero-row candidates, retained-agent historical DBs, global/default memory.db, and emergency handoff evidence.
The listed target files are not treated as active .mcp.json bindings or approved SQLite runtime exceptions.

The Cell permits only path, size, timestamp, table-count, row-count, and classification metadata and prohibits stored memory text inspection, export, reveal, migration, deletion, purge, rewrite, or semantic summarization.
hotel-lead and secretary historical DB review does not imply retained-agent host restart, binding mutation, DB migration, deletion, or active runtime state change.
The PR does not commit .mcp.json, Claude/Codex settings, env files, secrets, local DB files, or stored memory content.
Diff checks and changed YAML/JSON parse evidence are present and match the reported scope.
The PR does not authorize backend parity, common DB completion, UAMP conformance, compliance, federation, release, publish, DLP, zero-leakage, or legal/regulatory claims.
